Tests object property manipulation and string concatenation in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the use of object properties and basic assertions in JavaScript. It defines two objects, `a` and `b`, each with properties `n` and `s` intended to hold a `Number` and a `String`, respectively. The script assigns numeric values to `a.n` and `b.n`, and string values to `a.s` and `b.s`. It then uses an `assert` statement to verify that the sum of `a.n` and `b.n` equals 3, and the concatenation of `a.s` and `b.s` results in the string "qwerty". This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on basic operations with object properties and simple assertions.
